//: Shared strict TypeScript base. Every package/app extends exactly ONE config from @app/tsconfig (this base directly, or a runtime variant: nestjs.json / nextjs.json / node.json / library.json) so a single change propagates everywhere instead of being copied per app. Runtime variants override ONLY what their runtime needs (module system, JSX, emit vs no-emit).

compilerOptions.//incremental: WHY true here, but the buildable shared package (e.g. @app/contracts) MUST override this to false: with --incremental tsc writes tsconfig.tsbuildinfo OUTSIDE dist and then skips emit after dist is cleared, leaving consumers with no dist. A package that must ALWAYS emit sets incremental:false + composite:false. See packages/contracts/tsconfig.json.
